ddls:
CREATE TABLE "eye_tracking_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who generated the data */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session the data point belongs to */,
  "movement" TEXT /* Type of movement (e.g., transition, fixation) */,
  "time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "gaze_angle_x" REAL /* Gaze angle in the X direction */,
  "gaze_angle_y" REAL /* Gaze angle in the Y direction */,
  "euclidean_difference" REAL /* Euclidean difference between consecutive data points */,
  "temporal_difference" REAL /* Temporal difference between consecutive data points */,
  "pupil_dilation" REAL /* Pupil dilation */,
  "blink_rate" REAL /* Blink rate */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score for the data point */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device used to collect the data */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Information about the environment where the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_eye_tracking_data_movement FOREIGN KEY ("movement") REFERENCES movements ("movement_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_eye_tracking_data_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_eye_tracking_data_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department within the organization */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged into the system */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who conducted the session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  "session_description" TEXT /* Description of the session */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device used during the session */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Information about the environment where the session took place */,
  "session_type" TEXT /* Type of session (e.g., research, testing, development) */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in seconds */,
  "session_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movements" (
  "movement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movement type */,
  "movement_name" TEXT /* Name of the movement type (e.g., transition, fixation) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the movement type */,
  "movement_duration" REAL /* Average duration of the movement type */,
  "movement_frequency" REAL /* Frequency of the movement type in a typical session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to ensure data integrity */,
  "file_version" TEXT /* Version of the data file */,
  "file_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the session or file */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session being accessed */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the file being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the session or file was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Exact timestamp of the access event */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES data_files ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON string or text field detailing the permissions associated with the role */,
  "role_status" TEXT /* Status of the role (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "user_session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who conducted the session */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session */,
  "session_status" TEXT /* Status of the session (e.g., active, completed) */,
  "session_start_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the session was started */,
  "session_end_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the session was ended */,
  "session_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the user session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
